tooltips.Gig Worker: Struggle day and night for a couple of dollars. It feels like you are at the brink of burnout each day.
tooltips.Food Service: Serve coffee and food to grumpy customers. It's not much but it's honest work.
tooltips.Delivery Driver: Deliver packages around the city. A relaxing but still a poor paying job.
tooltips.Warehouse Worker: Lift heavy boxes in a massive warehouse. The pay is quite meager compared to the physical toll.
tooltips.Office Temp: File papers and make copies. A respectable and OK paying entry-level job.
tooltips.Personal Assistant: Manage a busy executive's schedule. The job pays decently well and is a lot less manually-intensive.
tooltips.Intern: Fetch coffee and run errands at a small publishing house. Very meager pay but the work experience is quite valuable.
tooltips.Blogger: Write articles for an online publication. A creative, respectable job but you are still unknown in the grand scheme of things.
tooltips.Content Creator: More experienced than the average blogger, you create engaging content for a growing audience. The pay is not that bad.
tooltips.Copy Editor: Proofread and polish manuscripts for other authors. A decently paying and very respectable job in the industry.
tooltips.Staff Writer: Utilising your unmatched writing ability, you produce high-quality articles on tight deadlines. Most bloggers never acquire such a well paying job.
tooltips.Senior Editor: Obliterate weak prose and plot holes in manuscripts with extraordinary proficiency. Such a feared editor is paid extremely well.
tooltips.Ghostwriter: Write entire books for famous clients under strict non-disclosure agreements. The few who attain this level of skill are showered with money.
tooltips.Full-Time Author: You've made it. Quit your day job to focus entirely on your masterpiece. No income, but provides a x5 multiplier to Writing Speed.
tooltips.C.W. Student: Study the theory of literature and practice creative writing exercises. There is minor pay to cover living costs.
tooltips.Junior Agent: Under the supervision of a Senior Agent, you discover new talent and negotiate small deals. Generous pay will be provided.
tooltips.Senior Agent: Turn the tides of an author's career by securing major publishing deals and mentoring other agents. The pay for this job is extremely high.
tooltips.Publisher: Utilise your vast network to acquire and publish books that shape the cultural landscape. Only a small percentage of agents attain this role.
tooltips.Literary Titan: Blessed with unparalleled talent, you can spot a bestseller from a mile away. It is said that a titan can make or break an entire genre.
tooltips.Head of Publishing: Spend your days administrating a global publishing empire and searching for the next literary movement. The Head receives ludicrous amounts of pay daily.
tooltips.Focus: Improve your learning speed through intense concentration.
tooltips.Time Management: Learn to procrastinate less and receive more job experience per day.
tooltips.Frugality: Study the tricks of budgeting to lower any type of expense.
tooltips.Meditation: Fill your mind with peace to tap into greater inspiration from within.
tooltips.Typing Speed: Practice your typing to get words on the page faster.
tooltips.Grammar & Prose: Study the rules of language to write more beautifully.
tooltips.Plotting: Strengthen your storytelling ability to craft compelling narratives.
Learn to create lifelike characters through habit and observation.
tooltips.Healthy Lifestyle: Lengthen your lifespan through diet and exercise. More time to write!
tooltips.Flow State: Bend your perception of time through deep, uninterrupted work.
tooltips.Longevity Secrets: Through harnessing modern science, lengthen your lifespan drastically.
tooltips.Brand Management: Encompass yourself with the power of your author brand, allowing you to absorb any job or skill with ease.
tooltips.Networking: Cultivate your industry connections, improving Fame gain between lifetimes.
tooltips.Public Speaking: Learn to command a room, striking better deals and earning heavy discounts.
tooltips.Personal Brand: A human mind is too feeble to grasp the market. Develop a powerful brand to absorb knowledge rapidly.
tooltips.Media Tours: Grow your fame through TV appearances and interviews, drastically increasing Fame gain.
tooltips.Royalty Negotiation: Through shrewd negotiation, multiply the value of contracts and advances you receive from your job.
tooltips.Homeless: Sleep on the streets or in shelters. It cannot get any worse than this.
tooltips.Rented Room: A small room in a shared house. Horrible conditions but at least you have a roof over your head.
tooltips.Studio Apartment: A single, small room that serves as your kitchen, bedroom, and office. The radiator is noisy.
tooltips.Suburban House: A small house in the suburbs. Provides decent living conditions for a fair price.
tooltips.Large House: A building with a few rooms and a yard. Although quite expensive, it is a comfortable abode.
tooltips.City Penthouse: Much larger than a regular house, boasting amazing city views. The building is quite spacious but comes with a hefty price tag.
tooltips.Private Villa: A very rich and meticulously built structure with a pool and guest house. Extremely high expenses for a lavish lifestyle.
tooltips.Mansion Estate: A grand residence with its own grounds and staff. Provides the utmost luxurious and comfortable living conditions for a ludicrous price.
tooltips.Library Card: Access to a world of information, allowing you to learn a lot more quickly.
tooltips.Used Laptop: A cheap computer used for writing exercises to improve your typing even faster than before.
tooltips.Editor: A freelance editor who assists with your work, giving you more time to be productive.
tooltips.Style Guide: A reference book for grammar and style, helping you gain experience in your creative work faster.
tooltips.Ergonomic Chair: A comfortable, supportive chair that keeps your body and mind fresh, leaving you in a happier, stress-free mood.
tooltips.Pro Writing Software: Powerful software with outlining and editing tools, providing a much easier time learning the craft.
tooltips.Home Office: A dedicated area which provides fine stationary and equipment designed for furthering your progress.
tooltips.Home Library: A personal collection of books, each containing vast amounts of information from life skills to literary theory.
